subject: SCIENCE
title: Classify the Animals
objective: Classify animals by observable traits and explain the chosen rule.
introduction: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Classify the Animals”.
A key word used in the lesson “Classify the Animals”.
A key word used in the lesson “Classify the Animals”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Which rule uses an observable trait?

feedback.correct: Correct: has feathers or no feathers
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. is nice or not nice and is lucky or unlucky do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Classify the Animals”?

options.text: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.

items.explanation: The correct response is “A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.
The correct response is “feathers/no feathers” because it follows the lesson explanation: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Group by favorite color” because it follows the lesson explanation: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.

items.prompt: Which rule uses an observable trait?
items.explanation: The correct response is “has feathers or no feathers” because it follows the lesson explanation: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “has scales/no scales” because it follows the lesson explanation: A learner-made group is an observation tool, not formal biological taxonomy.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Classify the Animals; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Classify the Animals.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
